signals.reasoning: BTC remains firm, but the edge is still too mild to justify a fresh direction under a caution regime.
ETH is still the cleanest long on the board because price and open interest are rising together while funding is flat, but caution keeps size and confidence restrained.
SOL still reads as a short-covering rally rather than a durable continuation entry, so I would keep it watch-only.
NOT is one of the better reduced-size squeeze longs because funding has flipped meaningfully negative while price is only modestly higher, but liquidity is poor so conviction stays capped.
PENGU has momentum but not enough structural edge or crowding to justify forcing a position in caution.
BRETT is too flat and too low-liquidity to justify a serious fresh trade.
SPX still qualifies as a secondary squeeze long because funding remains clearly negative while price and open interest are both rising, but caution keeps this reduced-confidence only.
GOAT is not showing enough signal quality or liquidity to deserve a fresh bet here.
TON is very extended and now funding is meaningfully positive, which makes it the clearest reduced-size peak-fade candidate on this board.
VVV remains strong and trend-supported enough that I would not short it without a clearer rejection, but it is also too extended to chase.
